summary: Updated the README progress badge target links so the Code Progress and Data Progress badges still render from progress.decomp.club shield endpoints, but clicking them now goes to the project's GitHub Pages progress page at https://doldecomp.github.io/melee/progress/ instead of the raw progress.decomp.club data page.
what_changed: Changed the click-through URL for the Code Progress badge in .github/README.md from https://progress.decomp.club/data/melee/ to https://doldecomp.github.io/melee/progress/.
Changed the click-through URL for the Data Progress badge in .github/README.md from https://progress.decomp.club/data/melee/ to https://doldecomp.github.io/melee/progress/.
Kept the badge image endpoints themselves unchanged: both badges still use img.shields.io endpoint URLs backed by progress.decomp.club with measure=code and measure=data.
smart_moves: Separated badge display data from badge destination: the shield endpoint remains the existing progress.decomp.club API, while the user-facing link now points to the more project-specific GitHub Pages progress page.
Made a minimal README-only change with two link-target edits and no unrelated documentation churn.
decomp_lessons: Progress badges can use progress.decomp.club as the machine-readable shield endpoint while directing users to a curated project progress page.
For repository-facing documentation, prefer stable user-facing project pages such as doldecomp.github.io/melee/progress/ when available, rather than raw progress data URLs.
Small metadata and documentation fixes help keep decompilation progress discoverable without touching code or matching-sensitive files.
naming_conventions: README badge labels use human-readable names: "Code Progress" and "Data Progress".
The shield endpoint distinguishes progress measures with query parameters measure=code and measure=data.
The project progress page path used by this PR is /melee/progress/ under doldecomp.github.io.
follow_up_queries: Find README badge link updates that point progress badges to doldecomp.github.io pages.
Search for progress.decomp.club links that still use raw data pages as click targets.
Check whether other doldecomp repositories use the same GitHub Pages progress URL convention.
